rationale: Both candidates independently converge on the same core structural nit (derive component list from _dit_modules instead of a hardcoded tuple), which echoes the spirit of dsocek's ground-truth question about whether the fix covers 'all potential cases.' X goes further and identifies that SDXL text-encoder LoRA weights are still a silent no-op — directly extending the PR's own stated risk ('adapters load/activate while applying to zero layers') to an uncovered case, which is the closest either candidate gets to dsocek's actual concern. Y is more exhaustively grounded (extensive consumer/grep sweep, MagiHuman sr_dit gap, registry.py divergence) and arguably edges X on precision, but its findings are duplication/DRY nits rather than coverage-completeness bugs. Neither surfaces the packed_modules_mapping/naming-conflict suggestion from ground truth, so recall is capped for both; X's text-encoder catch gives it a slight edge.
